analysis.reasoning: Core message: The tweet announces an exhibition and an online conference on the history of anti-Nazi resistance in Europe, organized by the Russian Historical Society. Evaluation: RU1 (West immoral/hostile/decadent) – Not promoted; no criticism of the West or allies. RU2 (Russia leads an alternative system) – Not present; no mention of multipolarity or alternative systems. RU3 (Russia is a benevolent partner) – Not directly; while it promotes an educational event, it does not frame Russia as aiding others or fostering peace/solidarity explicitly. RU4 (Russian history is admirable) – Partially relevant since it involves historical remembrance, but the focus is on European anti-Nazi resistance broadly, not specifically celebrating Russian historical achievements; the tweet does not highlight Russian contributions. RU5 (Russia at the forefront) – Not present. RU6 (Russia interesting culturally) – Slightly adjacent as a cultural event announcement, but the narrative emphasizes culture/nature/heritage appeal; this is a niche historical conference notice without broader cultural promotion of Russia. Conclusion: No listed narrative is clearly promoted.
